topic: gentle service of wine with residual carbon dioxide
trigger_keywords: servir vinos con gas carbónico residual

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A wine retains dissolved carbon dioxide when it is opened and poured.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Agitation and rapid pouring create nucleation and turbulence that release dissolved gas quickly.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Avoiding agitation and pouring slowly limits sudden foaming, overflow, and unnecessary loss of carbon dioxide.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Gentle handling and slow pouring control the release of dissolved carbon dioxide.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
